free_text: Fast neutron spectrum unfolding of a TRIGA Mark II
reactor and measurement of spectrum-averaged cross
sections: integral tests of differential cross
High purity foils of several metals, namely Al, Ti,
Fe, Ni,Nb, Mo, In and Au, were cut in circular discs
with diameter of 1 cm and placed in an Al-container.
A short 10 min irradiation of In and Au foils.
The spectrum of fast neutrons having energies
from 0.5 to 20 MeV was unfolded by threshold
Using the 58Ni(n,p)58Co reaction as monitor the flux
of neutrons above 0.5 MeV was determined to be about
9.98E+12 n/cm2/s at the reactor power of 1 MW.
The flux of thermal neutrons via the 197Au(n,g)198Au
